task: I’m working on a short school article and I have enough confirmed notes for it, but the draft feels a little plain. Can you help me make it read well without adding anything I can’t back up?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — send me the draft, and I’ll polish it without adding anything unverified.

I can help with:
- a stronger lead
- smoother transitions
- removing repetition
- cleaner, more neutral wording

If you want, I can do it in either of these formats:
1. **Light edit only** — keep your structure, just improve the wording
2. **Full polish** — rewrite it more cleanly while keeping every confirmed fact the same

Paste the draft whenever you’re ready.

user_simulator.text:
Here’s the draft. It’s short and I’m trying to keep it plain, not dramatic:

A burst water pipe in the school building delayed first period on Tuesday morning and closed part of the hallway near the science rooms. Students were told to wait in the main hallway while custodians cleaned up the water and staff checked the area. One science teacher said classes near the leak were moved or delayed for safety. A custodian said the cleanup took longer than expected because the water spread farther than it first looked. By around 8:20 a.m., students were allowed to move to class, but the schedule was still running late.

I want it to sound cleaner, just not fake or overdone.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a cleaner version that keeps the same facts and stays plain:

A burst water pipe in the school building delayed first period on Tuesday morning and closed part of the hallway near the science rooms. Students waited in the main hallway while custodians cleaned up the water and staff checked the area. One science teacher said classes near the leak were moved or delayed for safety. A custodian said the cleanup took longer than expected because the water spread farther than it first appeared. By about 8:20 a.m., students were allowed to move to class, although the schedule was still running late.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little more newsy
- even shorter
- or give you 2–3 alternate lead sentences to choose from

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. It’s cleaner but still plain, which is what I wanted.
